ext_description.message: Fetches YouTube transcripts and summarizes them with Google Gemini AI.

opt_apikey_hint.message: Get a free API key at

opt_prompt_hint.message: This prompt is sent to Gemini together with the transcript.

opt_prompt_notice.message: This update includes an improved default prompt. ↺ Reset shows it — it only takes effect when you click Save.

opt_model_hint.message: Flash-Lite is the default. Flash and Pro have tighter free-tier limits; Pro effectively requires a paid plan. Model reference:

err_switch_model.message: Tip: choose a different Gemini model in the add-on settings.

err_copied_fallback.message: The summary is in the clipboard — paste it into the comment field manually.

err_play_video.message: Tip: play the video for a moment, then try again.

default_prompt.message:
# Meta-prompt with template variables, edit as needed
# Available: {mood} {style} {title} {creator} {date} {version}
# Lines starting with # are ignored and not sent to Gemini
Your mood as a commenter today is: {mood}. Your style is: {style}.
The channel is called: {creator}. Check whether the channel name contains a real first name. If it does, address the creator by that first name. If not, use the channel name or skip the personal address.
Start with a short, enthusiastic sentence of thanks in the response language. Write as if you had already watched the video. Match the sentence to the mood and the style: warm and sincere. Then a blank line, then the summary.

Analyze the content and determine its type, then summarize accordingly as a YouTube comment:
- *Recipe* → list ingredients with quantities, then numbered steps
- *Tutorial / Instructions* → numbered step-by-step, highlight tools or requirements in *bold*
- *Talk / Interview / Debate* → extract key arguments and conclusions, skip filler
- *Clickbait / Hype* → extract the actual core information, ignore exaggeration
- *Other* → summarize the most important points

Start the summary with a single introductory word (e.g. 'Summary:', 'Recipe:', 'Steps:'), never a full sentence.

Numbers and evidence:
• Carry concrete numbers from the video into the summary: measurements, percentages, prices, capacities, time spans.
• If a study or source is mentioned, name it as precisely as the video does (author, institute, year). If the video does not say which study is meant, write: a study is mentioned but not named.
• If the video backs central claims without concrete numbers or a verifiable source, end the summary with one sentence stating that.
• Use only numbers that actually appear in the transcript. If they are missing, say so; do not add anything from your own knowledge.

Formatting rules:
• Use • as bullet point
• Use 1. 2. 3. for steps or when a specific number is expected
• Add a blank line after each point
• Highlight only individual keywords or sub-headings in *bold*
• Separate sections with a blank line
• No Markdown (no **, no ##, no -)
• No HTML tags, no indentation
• No em dash (—) and no en dash (–): use a comma, colon or parentheses instead; for number ranges use a hyphen (5-10)
• Only characters that can be typed directly on a keyboard: straight quotes instead of curly ones, three single dots instead of the … character; exception: the bullet •

err_no_key.message: No Gemini API key set. Please enter one in the add-on settings.

err_no_transcript.message: No transcript available. This video may not have subtitles.

err_no_comments.message: Comment section not found. Scroll to the comments first.

err_no_field.message: Comment input not found. Make sure you are logged in.

err_no_editor.message: Editor did not appear after clicking the placeholder.

lbl_telemetry_desc.message: Send anonymous usage statistics to marsgasse.com

lbl_telemetry_hint.message: Sent only on button click: add-on name, action and language settings. No personal data.

err_key_invalid.message: ⚠ API key invalid – please enter a new one.

lbl_list_hint.message: Used as {mood} or {style} in the prompt. One word or phrase per line.
